summary: Justin Brennan interviews Zarak Fatah, a life coach, about personal development, trauma healing, and relationship dynamics. The discussion focuses on mindset shifts, identifying limiting beliefs from childhood trauma, and achieving emotional fulfillment through inner work rather than external accumulation. No multifamily real estate content is presented.
takeaways: Write down anxious thoughts to validate whether fears are true or rooted in old stories; ask 'Do I have proof?' before accepting negative beliefs

Use a three-step state-shifting routine: (1) list three things you're grateful for, (2) ground barefoot on soil/grass facing light with hand over heart for five deep breaths, (3) listen to a high-vibe playlist of five anchor songs
Identify originating incidents of limiting beliefs through hypnotherapy or breath work, then reframe childhood trauma from adult consciousness to release behavioral patterns
Core needs: women need to feel provided for, protected, and emotionally safe; men need a supportive spouse, peace in home, and physical intimacy
Recognize that 95% of actions are controlled by subconscious beliefs; make the unconscious conscious through journaling to consciously choose different behaviors
Masculine-feminine polarity in relationships: when sexual polarity drops, emotional safety/intimacy disconnects and woman pulls away, triggering anxious attachment in man
Never rob someone of rock bottom—adversity and disruptive events create perspective shifts needed for transformation and healing
title: I Stopped Listening to Broke People and Got Rich
